steps.title: Find what the sentence is doing
steps.content:
The first sentence establishes that the differences between the two editions are extreme — remarkable even by James's standards. The sentence being completed then presents critics regarding the two editions as entirely different novels. This second sentence doesn't contradict or restate the first; it reinforces it by showing the real-world consequence of those extreme differences.
  • "by contrast,": signals opposition ✗
  • "in fact,": signals reinforcement or intensification of the preceding point ✓
  • "nevertheless,": signals concession, implying the critics' view is surprising despite the differences ✗
  • "in other words,": signals restatement, implying the two clauses say the same thing ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “in fact” correctly presents the critics' view as a natural extension of the extreme differences already established. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
